title: parsonsia corymbosa
intro: Parsonsia corymbosa, also known as the Parson's Fingers or Fingers Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of various alkaloids. The health benefits of Parsonsia corymbosa are believed to include relief from fever, headaches, and respiratory issues, as well as antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Parsonsia corymbosa are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using the plant's roots to create a decoction.
